metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes; daemon_capability: full (parser now skips injected:true `!`-shell escape lines — fix 5e0b4f5c — so the session no longer sticks in working); driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: gap:wait_turn
route: driver-gap (record ports wait_turn, then drives — no longer known_failing)

RE-ASSESSMENT after fix 5e0b4f5c. The prior verdict was daemon=bug (the injected `!`-shell escape result opened a turn that never closed → the session stuck in working). That defect is now FIXED in the parser; the daemon pillar is FULL.

## agent — yes

Mistral Vibe's TUI has a `!`-shell escape. Typing `!echo hello` at the REPL prompt runs the command LOCALLY as a subprocess (`asyncio.create_subprocess_shell`, cli/textual_ui/app.py:1531), with NO LLM round-trip / no `act()` turn. On completion the TUI calls `agent_loop.inject_user_context(...)` (app.py:1574-1580) which appends an `injected=True` user `LLMMessage` and immediately `_save_messages()` (core/agent_loop.py:751-777), so the result lands in `messages.jsonl` right away as a single line. The exact on-disk shape is confirmed in three live transcripts (`grep -l '"injected": true' ~/.vibe/logs/session/*/messages.jsonl`): `{"role":"user","content":"Manual `!` command result from the user. Use this as context only.…","injected":true,…}`. The literal template is in cli/textual_ui/app.py:1645-1665 (`_format_manual_command_context`).

## daemon — full (was bug; fixed by 5e0b4f5c)

The vibe parser now SPECIAL-CASES `injected:true` user lines and SKIPS them (parser.go:59-71: `if injected, _ := raw["injected"].(bool); injected { ev.Skip = true; return ev }`). A skipped event is not transcript activity, so state_classifier rule 4 never fires ready→working for a bare `!cmd`: the session STAYS in ready throughout the shell-escape window. This is the exact behavior the scenario asserts — process step 5 'State returns to ready' and the failure-mode bullet 'State does not stick in working after the shell command exits' — and it mirrors the claudecode sibling, which SKIPS the `<bash-input>`/`<bash-stdout>` wrappers for the same reason (claudecode/parser.go:216-225). The injected line is explicitly labelled 'Use this as context only' — deferred context for the NEXT real turn — and the parser now honors that. The real prompt that follows (injected:false) drives the ordinary ready→working→ready arc, proving the session is still alive. Spec-required observable now handled correctly → daemon=full.

## driver — gap:wait_turn

The `!echo hello` shell escape is a plain `send` (send_text does `tmux send-keys -l` + Enter; the leading `!` is intercepted by the REPL — no `keys` primitive needed). But the recipe's prove-alive turn ('Reply with exactly the word: alive') needs `wait_turn` to reliably observe the ready→working→ready arc, and the scaffolded driver STILL STUBS wait_turn: DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep" (driver-interactive.sh:113) omits it, and SEAM 2 (lines 168-170) calls `not_implemented wait_turn`. This is the same baseline column gap every other mistral-vibe cell carries; `record` ports SEAMs 1/2/3 before driving.

## route

agent=yes + daemon=full + driver=gap:wait_turn → routing row 'yes | full/bug | gap:* → driver-gap': `record` ports wait_turn, then drives, then records a NORMAL (passing) recording — the known_failing / issue-filing framing is retired.
caveats: PROVISIONAL on emission ORDERING: the parser+source read proves the injected `!` line is written immediately and (now) skipped, but whether a transient proc-<PID> presession row precedes the session_<ts>_<hash> birth is emission-dependent — session_birth is anchored to start UNPINNED so either satisfies it; record settles it.

daemon=full is verified from the fixed parser code (5e0b4f5c, parser.go:59-71) and parser_test.go's injected-skip test, but the live end-to-end no-stick behavior is confirmed by record, not by this doc read.
cost is not asserted: the vibe adapter never reads stats.session_cost and mistral-medium-3.5 is absent from core/pkg/capacity, so per-turn cost is not emitted (cost_nonzero=false).

sources.note: Lines 59-71: role "user" with `injected:true` → ev.Skip=true and early return (the fix). The doc-comment (lines 12-15, 59-67) now states injected `!`-shell escape results are context for the NEXT real turn and are SKIPPED so the session does not stick in working.
The injected-user test now asserts an injected `!` result is SKIPPED (not emitted as user_message) — pinning the fixed behavior.
fix(vibe): skip injected `!`-shell escape lines (was sticking session in working) — the commit that flips this cell's daemon pillar from bug to full.

Lines 1531 (local `asyncio.create_subprocess_shell`), 1574-1580 (`inject_user_context` on completion), 1645-1665 (`_format_manual_command_context` — the exact 'Manual `!` command result from the user. Use this as context only.' template). vibe 2.19.0.

Lines 751-777 (`inject_user_context`): appends an injected=True user LLMMessage and immediately `_save_messages()` to disk — NO act()/LLM turn is triggered, so a bare `!cmd` writes exactly one injected line (which the parser now skips).

Lines 216-225 (isLocalCommandContent): claudecode SKIPS `<bash-input>`/`<bash-stdout>` shell-escape wrappers so state stays ready — the same pattern the vibe parser now applies to its injected:true `!`-command results.

Line 113 DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep" (omits wait_turn) and lines 168-170 SEAM 2 wait_turn() → not_implemented — the driver still stubs wait_turn, so driver=gap:wait_turn. record ports it before driving.

preconditions: vibe CLI (2.19.0) on PATH and authenticated
irrlichd is recording (isolated mode or --attach against a --record daemon)
vibe launched in interactive TUI mode so the `!` shell-escape is available
driver's wait_turn SEAM ported from a reference driver before recording (baseline column gap)
setup: Driver launches vibe in a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/; session id derives from the session_<ts>_<hash> directory

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: alive

verify: events.jsonl shows the session entering ready after launch
during the ~6s window after `!echo hello`, NO state_transition to working (the parser skips the injected `!` result; state stays ready) and no transition to waiting
after the normal user prompt, events.jsonl shows ready→working→ready for the session (proves the session is still alive)
messages.jsonl contains the injected `!`-command result line plus the real user prompt and at least one assistant line for the final turn
